author: Scott S
executive_summary: A retail investor strongly opposes the proposed rule change, arguing it would benefit large market makers Citadel and Virtu at the expense of retail investors. The commenter expresses deep distrust of the SEC, characterizing the agency as captured by Wall Street interests.

positions.order_competition_rule: The commenter opposes repeal of the referenced regulation, viewing it as protective of retail investors
positions.best_execution: Implicitly concerned that repeal harms retail execution quality

economic_arguments: Repeal of the regulation would financially benefit dominant market makers Citadel and Virtu
Retail investors would be economically harmed by the proposed repeal
competition_arguments: Implicitly argues that the rule change favors entrenched large market makers over broader market participants
retail_investor_arguments: Retail investors would be disadvantaged by the repeal
The SEC is perceived as prioritizing Wall Street interests over retail investor protection
recommended_actions: Do not repeal the referenced regulation
key_quotes: It could not be more obvious that repealing this regulation would benefit Citadel & Virtu and screw retail investors.
I suppose that IS the ethos of the SEC; screw retail & suck off wall street
